codeReview.summary: The feature materially improves VAL-TOKEN-021 coverage by adding market size, revenue, demand, timeline, and Dune-backed prediction-market data to the token-utility surface, and the worker followed the content-strategy procedure in the transcript. However, one user-facing use case is wired to the wrong analysis: the Whale Exclusive Products card now renders enterprise data licensing market/revenue/timeline content, which makes the economic thesis internally inconsistent and means the surface does not fully satisfy VAL-TOKEN-021.

issues.description: The `util-whale-exclusives` analysis is actually Enterprise Data Licensing copy (`Enterprise blockchain market`, `Data licensing subscriptions`, planned institutional rollout), but it is rendered under the `Whale Exclusive Products` card defined in `src/components/tokenomics/TokenUtilitySurface.tsx:184-193`. That conflicts with the existing product taxonomy, which already separates `Whale Premium Products` from `Enterprise & API Rails` in `src/content/token-tiers.ts:665-705`. As shipped, users see enterprise-data TAM/revenue/timeline claims under a whale-exclusive card title, so one of the headline use-case analyses is not internally consistent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no documented crosswalk between the research doc's use-case sections and the utility-surface IDs, which makes it easy to attach the wrong analysis to a card and leaves it ambiguous how sections like Enterprise Data Licensing, Cross-Chain Expansion, and OpenServ/BRAID should map into the UI.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/use-case-analysis.md:366-458` defines separate sections for Enterprise Data Licensing, Cross-Chain Expansion, and OpenServ/BRAID Integration, while `src/components/tokenomics/TokenUtilitySurface.tsx:184-193` defines `util-whale-exclusives` as whale-only premium products and `src/content/use-case-analyses.ts:286-307` attaches enterprise-data analysis to that id.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared-state docs correctly note that `SourceCue.note` is not rendered, but the new use-case work still stores most citation detail only in `note` fields. That means future workers can think they added visible source citations when the UI actually shows only a coarse label unless an `href` is also supplied.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/content-model.md:37` says `SourceCue.note` is data-only metadata; `src/components/EvidenceHook.tsx:20-31` renders only label/asOf/href state; `src/content/use-case-analyses.ts:42-43` is the only use-case source with an `href`, while the remaining entries rely on non-rendered notes.

summary: Fail. The commit adds substantial VAL-TOKEN-021 depth and Dune-backed prediction-market data, but the Whale Exclusive Products card is populated with enterprise data licensing analysis, creating a user-visible internal inconsistency that blocks approval. Review report written to the scrutiny reviews directory.
